name: Google Workspace APIs
description: Calendar, Gmail, Drive, and Chat through the GA Google REST APIs — one OAuth flow, no Developer Preview required. Read/write with curated tools.
instructions:
One connector for four Google products via their GA REST APIs (no MCP preview program needed). OAuth runs as the user; every calendar/file/message they can access is available, limited by the granted scopes.

**Scopes requested:** `calendar` (read/write), `gmail.modify` (read, label, draft, send — no permanent delete), `drive` (read/write files), `chat.messages` (read + send).

**Setup — Google Cloud (one-time):**
1. Enable APIs: Google Calendar API, Gmail API, Google Drive API, Google Chat API.
2. OAuth consent screen (External) → add the four scopes above → add yourself as a Test user.
3. Web application OAuth client → redirect URI `https://<your-anythingmcp>/api/mcp-oauth/callback`.
4. Import this adapter with the client ID/secret, then **Authorize with Provider**.

**Calendar:** `gcal_list_calendars` shows every calendar on the user's list including shared ones (a spouse's calendar appears if shared). Writes need `accessRole` writer/owner on that calendar. Use `calendarId=primary` or an id from the list (often an email address).

**Gmail:** `gmail_search_messages` uses Gmail query syntax (`from:`, `subject:`, `newer_than:7d`). `gmail_create_draft` and `gmail_send_message` take a `raw` RFC 2822 message encoded base64url — construct headers (To/Subject) + body, then base64url-encode. Prefer drafts over direct send.

**Drive:** `drive_search_files` uses Drive query syntax (`name contains 'x'`, `fullText contains 'x'`, `mimeType = 'application/vnd.google-apps.document'`). `drive_export_file` converts Google Docs/Sheets to text/csv for reading. `drive_create_file` creates metadata-only files (e.g. an empty Google Doc or a folder with `mimeType application/vnd.google-apps.folder`); content upload is out of scope.

**Chat:** requires a Chat app configured in the GCP project (Google Chat API → Configuration) even for user-auth reads. If Chat tools return 403 PERMISSION_DENIED mentioning app config, complete that step or skip Chat.

**Write safety:** propose mutations (event create/update/delete, drafts, sends, file changes) before calling unless the user's current message explicitly authorizes that exact change.

**Rate limits:** per-product quotas; paginate with pageToken and avoid tight loops.

tools.name: gcal_list_calendars
tools.description: List all calendars on the user's calendar list — primary, secondary, and calendars shared with them. Returns id, summary, timeZone, accessRole (owner/writer/reader), primary.

minAccessRole.description: Filter: freeBusyReader, reader, writer, or owner.

tools.name: gcal_list_events
tools.description: List events on one calendar. Use calendarId=primary or an id from gcal_list_calendars (including shared calendars).

singleEvents.description: Expand recurring events (use true with orderBy=startTime).

q.description: Free-text search over title, description, location, attendees.

tools.name: gcal_get_event
tools.description: Fetch a single calendar event by calendarId + eventId.

tools.name: gcal_create_event
tools.description: Create a calendar event. Needs writer/owner accessRole. Timed events: startDateTime + endDateTime (+ timeZone). All-day: startDate + endDate (YYYY-MM-DD, end exclusive).

tools.name: gcal_update_event
tools.description: Patch an existing event — include only fields to change. Same start/end rules as gcal_create_event.

tools.name: gcal_delete_event
tools.description: Delete a calendar event. Needs writer/owner access.

tools.name: gmail_search_messages
tools.description: Search Gmail with query syntax: from:, to:, subject:, newer_than:7d, has:attachment, label:, is:unread. Returns message ids — fetch details with gmail_get_message or gmail_get_thread.

q.description: Gmail search query, e.g. "from:jane subject:invoice newer_than:30d".

labelIds.description: Comma-free single label id, e.g. INBOX or UNREAD.

tools.name: gmail_get_message
tools.description: Fetch one Gmail message. format=metadata returns headers only (fast); format=full includes the body payload (base64url parts).

tools.name: gmail_get_thread
tools.description: Fetch a full Gmail thread (all messages) by thread id.

tools.name: gmail_list_labels
tools.description: List Gmail labels (system + user) with ids for use in search and label changes.

tools.name: gmail_modify_labels
tools.description: Add/remove labels on a message — archive (remove INBOX), mark read (remove UNREAD), star (add STARRED), or apply user labels.

tools.name: gmail_create_draft
tools.description:
Create a Gmail draft. raw = base64url-encoded RFC 2822 message: build "To: a@b.com
Subject: Hi
Content-Type: text/plain; charset=utf-8

Body text", then base64url-encode it. Prefer drafts over gmail_send_message so the user reviews before sending.

raw.description: Base64url-encoded RFC 2822 message (headers + blank line + body).

tools.name: gmail_send_message
tools.description: Send an email immediately (no draft review). Same raw base64url RFC 2822 format as gmail_create_draft. Only on explicit user instruction to send.

tools.name: drive_search_files
tools.description: Search Drive with query syntax: name contains 'x', fullText contains 'x', mimeType = 'application/vnd.google-apps.document', modifiedTime > '2026-01-01T00:00:00', 'folderId' in parents, trashed = false.

q.description: Drive query, e.g. "name contains 'budget' and trashed = false".

tools.name: drive_get_file
tools.description: Fetch file metadata by id (name, mimeType, size, webViewLink, parents, sharing).

tools.name: drive_export_file
tools.description: Export a Google Doc/Sheet/Slides as readable text. mimeType: text/plain (Docs), text/csv (Sheets), text/plain (Slides). Only works on Google-native files; use drive_get_file webViewLink for binaries.

mimeType.description: Export format, e.g. text/plain or text/csv.

tools.name: drive_create_file
tools.description: Create a metadata-only Drive file: empty Google Doc (mimeType application/vnd.google-apps.document), Sheet (...spreadsheet), or folder (...folder). Content upload not supported here.

tools.name: drive_update_file
tools.description: Rename, describe, or trash a Drive file (metadata patch).

trashed.description: true moves the file to trash.

tools.name: chat_list_spaces
tools.description: List Google Chat spaces (rooms and DMs) the user belongs to. Requires a Chat app configured in the GCP project — if this 403s, skip Chat.

tools.name: chat_list_messages
tools.description: List messages in a Chat space (newest first with orderBy createTime desc).

spaceId.description: Space id — the part after 'spaces/' in the resource name from chat_list_spaces, e.g. AAAA1234.

tools.name: chat_send_message
tools.description: Send a text message to a Chat space as the user. Only on explicit user instruction.

spaceId.description: Space id — the part after 'spaces/' in the resource name, e.g. AAAA1234.
